rulings.Case Title: KEVIN WILLIAMS VS. ERIC SAFIRE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Justice William Stein'S Appointment As Arb
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 21, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANTS ERIC SAFIRE AND JOHN SCOTT'S Petition To Confirm Justice William Stein'S Appointment As Arbitrator is Granted. Since Ruth Glick is unable to serve as the arbitrator, arbitrate this matter, Justice William Stein (retired) is appointed the arbitrator per the court order issued March 22, 2012. Mr. Williams' objections are not well-founded as he has already used his one peremptory challenge and has not shown good cause to disqualify Justice Stein. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: SHERRI WATSON VS. ROBERT G GISH M.D.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production And Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday March 21, 2012, line 1. DEFENDANT NOVARTIS PHARMACEUTICALS CORPORATION Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production And Interrogatories - Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.= 302/HK

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES C. BOILLOD VS. CHRISTINA M. WENDEL PHD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 21, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT WILLIAM QUICK MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - Motion for summary judgment is denied and alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted on the third cause of action for breach of trust and the fourth cause of action for fraud. There is a triable issue whether Dr. Quick breached the standard of care and whether that breach caused plaintiff damages. As to the breach of trust claim, Dr. Quick satisfied his burden of production and plaintiff has not presented any evidence showing that defendant was placed his financial interests over the interests of plaintiff. As to the fraud claim, Dr. Quick satisfied his burden of production and plaintiff has not presented any evidence showing that defendant had a fraudulent intent. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM PETER HANSEN DDS VS. OHIO NATIONAL LIFE ASSURANCE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omiton To Quash Pltfs' Depiosition Notices, Or Alternatively, For Protective Order; Memo Of P&A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 21, 2012, Line 19, DEFENDANT OHIO NATIONAL LIFE ASSURANCE CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Omiton To Quash Pltfs' Depiosition Notices, Or Alternatively, For Protective Order;The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin as follows: Defendant's motion for a protective order is GRANTED on the condition that Defendant pay for Plaintiff and Plaintiff's counsel's airfare and hotel expenses for a trip to Ohio, or the expenses of Plaintiff's counsel for such a trip and the expense of a video-conference feed if Plaintiff wants to attend the deposition but is unable to travel due to his condition. If Defendant is not willing to pay these expenses, the deposition shall take place in San Francisco. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
